data.title: U.S. national debt reaches record $38 trillion. Each American now owes $110,649. Debt increased from $36T in Nov 2024 to $38T in just two months. Fastest trillion added since pandemic.
data.description: Earlier this week, the American national debt hit a record high of $38 trillion, marking a rapid increase from $36 trillion in November 2024 to $37 trillion in August 2025. This $1 trillion increase in just two months underscores the acceleration of federal borrowing. It is noted that this is the fastest the country has added a trillion dollars to the national debt outside of the COVID-19 pandemic. These debt levels surpass the projections made by the Congressional Budget Office prior to 2030, which were issued before the pandemic. The borrowing surge post-pandemic aimed to enhance the economic recovery. Each American, when considering the total debt, would now owe a significant amount.

data.title: Commercial National Financial Corp reported Q3 2025 net income of $1,791,000 ($0.45 per share), up from Q3 2024's $1,408,000 ($0.36 per share). ROE rose to 13.35% from 11.32% in Q3 2024.
data.description: In Ithaca, Michigan, on October 24, 2025, Commercial National Financial Corporation (OTCID: CEFC) disclosed a third-quarter 2025 net income of $1,791,000, equivalent to $0.45 per share. This marked an increase from the third quarter of 2024, which saw a net income of $1,408,000 or $0.36 per share. The Return on Equity for the third quarter of 2025 stood at 13.35%, reflecting growth compared to the 11.32% reported for the third quarter of 2024.

data.title: Brompton Split Corp. Class A Share ETF's monthly distribution increased to $0.13 per unit, reflecting a 13% annualized rise.
data.description: Toronto, October 24, 2025 - Brompton Funds ("Brompton") has raised the monthly distribution for Brompton Split Corp. Class A Share ETF (TSX: CLSA) to $0.13 per unit for record dates between October and December 2025. This increase comes following strong growth in net asset value ("NAV") since the ETF's launch. The new distribution marks a 13% annualized rise from third quarter distributions, reflecting positive performance.

data.title: At Home Group Inc. completes financial restructuring, emerges from Chapter 11, gains new owners, and strengthened financial position.
data.description: At Home Group Inc. has officially completed its financial restructuring and emerged from Chapter 11 in Dallas. The Company is now entering a phase of renewal and profitable growth after finalizing the court-supervised process. By doing so, At Home significantly bolsters its financial standing, gains additional resources for strategic investments, and secures backing from new owners. The restructuring effort has resulted in the elimination of undisclosed figures.

data.title: LaToya C. James named Inner Circle Lifetime Member for contributions to IT. VP recognized for Driving Transformation.
data.description: In Burtonsville, Maryland, on October 24, 2025, LaToya C. James, VP, was acknowledged as an esteemed Inner Circle Lifetime Member in The Inner Circle for her noteworthy achievements in Driving Transformation and Strategic Growth within the Global IT sector. LaToya C. James has been commended for her exceptional contributions to the industry.

data.title: Martin D. Peacock, retired CEO of Peacock Towing Inc., recognized as Pinnacle Life Achiever by The Inner Circle.
data.description: In Americus, Georgia on October 24, 2025, Martin D. Peacock, former CEO of Peacock Towing Inc., was recognized as a Pinnacle Life Achiever in The Inner Circle for his noteworthy contributions to Driving Excellence in Business Operations. Peacock, who currently serves as the CEO, received this prestigious acknowledgment for his achievements in the industry.

data.title: Western Alliance Bank finances 192 affordable apartments in North Las Vegas, investing $43.8 million in tax-exempt bonds and 49% of $35.1 million in tax credit equity.
data.description: Western Alliance Bank revealed their involvement in financing the Senator Joseph M. Neal Jr. Apartments, a 192-unit affordable housing complex in North Las Vegas. The bank contributed by acquiring $43.8 million in tax-exempt construction bonds and investing 49% of the $35.1 million tax credit equity. Situated at 1632 Yale St. close to E. Tonopah Avenue and N. 5th Street, this four-story community aims to provide affordable housing in the area.

data.title: Waterton and The NRP Group team up to develop 368-unit South Valley Apartments in suburban Las Vegas, NV, close to employment options.
data.description: Ideal location provides easy access to a variety of job opportunities in a booming neighborhood located south of the Las Vegas Strip. Waterton, a renowned real estate investor and operator, has teamed up with The NRP Group, a comprehensive developer and builder.
